adjusted.reasoning: Anchor to market (~0.83/0.12/0.05); trim model's 0.92 toward the book. Portugal only drew their opener 1-1 vs DR Congo and were 'underwhelming', so the model's lambda 3.7 is too hot. Ronaldo starts and Portugal need a win, but the strong Under-2.5 lean (Portugal finishing concerns + Uzbekistan's conservative 3-4-2-1 block; Uzbekistan lost 1-3 to Colombia) caps the goal expectation. Expect a controlled 2-0 type win, not a rout.

factors.factor: Portugal drew opener 1-1 vs DR Congo, under pressure, 'underwhelming'
Ronaldo starts; predicted XI; Under 2.5 lean given attacking concerns

odds.source: Bookmaker consensus via web search 2026-06-23 (covers/OneFootball; POR -1.5 ~ -150, Under 2.5 lean)

adjusted.reasoning: Nudge slightly above market (~0.78) toward the supercomputer's 85% on the strength of England's opener (4-2 vs Croatia, 2.12 xG, 20 shots). Trim model's 0.97 down because Ghana are not a pushover: they won 1-0 vs Panama with a clean sheet and sit level on points, with first in Group L at stake. England xG ~2.1 -> modal 2-0; Ghana organized enough to keep it from a blowout.

adjusted.reasoning: Anchor to market (Croatia ~0.63, supercomputer 64.9%); trim model's 0.83 on Croatia. Both lost openers and must win, so the loser is effectively out. Croatia are favoured but limp in (lost 3 of last 4; conceded 4 to England) while Panama at neutral Toronto are unbeaten in 4 straight after a loss. Elevated draw chance (0.24) and a strong Under-2.5 lean point to a tight Croatia 0-1 win rather than a comfortable margin.

adjusted.reasoning: Anchor to market (Colombia ~0.63); trim model's 0.95 sharply. Colombia won 3-1 vs Uzbekistan with Luis Diaz starring (the Feb injury note is stale - he is fit and started), so Colombia are deserved favourites. But DR Congo are a well-organised Desabre side who drew Portugal, 'have not lost by more than a single goal', and sit deep to exploit transitions; strong Under-2.5 (-155). Expect a narrow 1-0 Colombia win with a real draw risk (0.25).

factors.factor: Colombia won 3-1 vs Uzbekistan, Luis Diaz fit and outstanding; -195 favourite
DR Congo drew Portugal, resilient deep block, 'not lost by more than a single goal'; Under 2.5 -155
